# @cdlab/tsconfig usage guide for AI agents

`@cdlab/tsconfig` is a config-only workspace package: one strict `base.json` plus
four framework overlays (`nextjs`, `hono`, `react-library`, `utils`). Each overlay
extends `base.json` and patches only the module system, JSX runtime, and emit
flags that differ per project type. There is **no runtime code, no build step, no
entry point, no dependencies** — consumers extend a preset by raw subpath and
`tsc` merges the options.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`. There are no scripts to run in this
package; validate changes by type-checking a consumer (see Common commands).

## What it is

- **Five JSON files at the package root**, no subdirs: `base.json`, `nextjs.json`,
  `hono.json`, `react-library.json`, `utils.json`.
- **No `exports` field** in `package.json` (load-bearing) — so
  `@cdlab/tsconfig/nextjs.json` resolves straight to the file at the package root.
- `package.json` has no `main`/`module`/`scripts`/`deps`. `private: true`
  (workspace-internal); `publishConfig.access: public` is vestigial and inert.

## Preset hierarchy (single level)

`base.json` extends nothing; each overlay `extends: "./base.json"`. No overlay
extends another. Merge order is base → overlay → consumer, key-by-key; **arrays
(`lib`, `types`, `plugins`) replace wholesale, they do not concatenate**.

- `base.json` — `strict`, `module`/`moduleResolution: NodeNext`, `target: ES2017`,
  `lib: [dom, dom.iterable, esnext]`, `moduleDetection: force`, `declaration` +
  `declarationMap: true`, `incremental: false`, `resolveJsonModule`, `skipLibCheck`,
  `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: false`.
- `nextjs.json` — `module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`, `jsx: preserve`,
  `noEmit: true`, `allowJs: true`, `plugins: [{ name: "next" }]`.
- `hono.json` — `target`+`module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`,
  `lib: [ESNext]` (drops DOM), `jsx: react-jsx` + `jsxImportSource: hono/jsx`.
  Re-states `strict`/`skipLibCheck` (redundant noise).
- `react-library.json` — `module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`,
  `jsx: react-jsx`. No plugin.
- `utils.json` — `module: ESNext`, `moduleResolution: Bundler`, `target: ES6`
  (lower than base on purpose), `types: [node]`, emit inverted:
  `noEmit: false`, `declaration: false`, `declarationMap: false` (tsdown emits types).

## Consumer inventory (authoritative)

- `nextjs.json` → `bycut`, `byplay`, `byshot`, `bytts`, `clearify`, `dropply-web`,
  `flnk`, `flox`, `SecureC`, `text2img`, `value-vision`, `vidl`, `wepush`.
- `hono.json` → `baccarat`, `byplay-log`, `dropply-api`, `live-user`.
- `react-library.json` → `packages/ui`.
- `utils.json` → `packages/utils`, `packages/cipher`, `packages/uncrypto`, `packages/db`.
- `base.json` → everything, directly or transitively.

## How consumers extend

```json
{
  "extends": "@cdlab/tsconfig/nextjs.json",
  "compilerOptions": { "paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] } },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

Presets never set `paths`/`include`/`exclude` — those are the consumer's job.

## Gotchas for agents

- **Do NOT add an `exports` field** to `package.json` — it breaks every consumer's
  raw-subpath `extends` string.
- **Arrays replace on merge.** Changing `base.json`'s `lib` affects every consumer
  except `hono.json` (which re-declares `lib`). Same for `types`/`plugins`.
- **base declares NodeNext, but all four overlays flip to Bundler** — so almost no
  consumer runs on NodeNext. It only matters for anything extending `base` directly.
- **The JSX runtime is the main differentiator**: `preserve` (nextjs), `react-jsx`
  (react-library + hono, hono adds `jsxImportSource: hono/jsx`), none (base/utils).
- `hono.json` drops DOM libs on purpose (Workers have no DOM); don't "fix" it.
- `utils.json` targets ES6 and disables declaration on purpose (tsdown owns emit);
  don't "align" it back to base.
- `private: true` wins over `publishConfig.access: public`; the package is not
  published.
- There are **no scripts** in this package — never invent `build`/`test`/`dev` for
  `@cdlab/tsconfig`.

## Common commands

Type-check a consumer to validate a preset change (there is nothing to run here):

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/wepush build         # exercises nextjs.json
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-api build    # exercises hono.json
pnpm --filter @cdlab/utils build          # exercises utils.json
```

`react-library.json` has no script — `packages/ui` ships raw source with no
build step; validate it by type-checking an app that imports `@cdlab/ui`.
